title: नरसिंह पञ्चामृत स्तोत्रम्
devanagariText:
॥ श्री नरसिंह पञ्चामृत स्तोत्रम् ॥

अहोबलं नरसिंहं गुहाबलं नरसिंहम्।
क्रोडाचलं नरसिंहं भार्गवं नरसिंहम्।
योगानन्दं नरसिंहं छत्रवटं नरसिंहम्।
पावनं नरसिंहं कारञ्जं नरसिंहम्।
श्रीकृष्णस्थलं नरसिंहं नमामि नरसिंहम्॥ १॥

त्वत्प्रभुजीवप्रियमिच्छसि चेन्नरहरिपूजां कुरु सततम्।
प्रतिबिम्बालङ्कृतिधृतकुसुमस्तबकमनोहरम्॥ २॥

उग्रं वीरं महाविष्णुं ज्वलन्तं सर्वतोमुखम्।
नृसिंहं भीषणं भद्रं मृत्युमृत्युं नमाम्यहम्॥ ३॥

प्रह्लादवरदं श्रीशं हिरण्यकशिपोर्हरम्।
करालवदनं रौद्रं नरसिंहं नमाम्यहम्॥ ४॥

सत्त्वं रजस्तमश्चेति प्रकृतेः सम्भवा गुणाः।
तेषामतीतमात्मानं नृसिंहं नित्यमाश्रये॥ ५॥

वज्रनखं वज्रदंष्ट्रं वज्रदेहं महाबलम्।
वज्रोद्भवं च विज्ञेयं नृसिंहं कामदं भजे॥ ६॥

योगानन्दं नरसिंहं ध्यायेन्नित्यं समाहितः।
सर्वपापक्षयकरं सर्वसिद्धिप्रदायकम्॥ ७॥

सर्वरक्षाकरं सत्यं सत्यं सर्वगतं विभुम्।
पञ्चामृतस्तवं पठेद्यः स तीर्थफलमाप्नुयात्॥ ८॥

॥ इति श्री नरसिंह पञ्चामृत स्तोत्रम् सम्पूर्णम् ॥
transliteration:
|| Shri Narasimha Panchamrita Stotram ||

Ahobalam Narasimham Guhabalam Narasimham |
Krodachalam Narasimham Bhargavam Narasimham |
Yoganandam Narasimham Chhatravatam Narasimham |
Pavanam Narasimham Karanjam Narasimham |
Shri Krishnasthalam Narasimham Namami Narasimham || 1 ||

Tvat Prabhu Jiva Priyam Ichchhasi Chen Narahari Pujam Kuru Satatam |
Pratibimba Alankriti Dhrita Kusuma Stabaka Manoharam || 2 ||

Ugram Viram Mahavishnum Jvalantam Sarvatomukham |
Nrisimham Bhishanam Bhadram Mrityu Mrityum Namamy Aham || 3 ||

Prahladavaradam Shrisham Hiranyakashipor Haram |
Karalavadanam Raudram Narasimham Namamy Aham || 4 ||

Sattvam Rajas Tamashcheti Prakriteh Sambhava Gunah |
Tesham Atitam Atmanam Nrisimham Nityam Ashraye || 5 ||

Vajranakham Vajradamshtram Vajradeham Mahabalam |
Vajrodbhavam Cha Vijneyam Nrisimham Kamadam Bhaje || 6 ||

Yoganandam Narasimham Dhyayen Nityam Samahitah |
Sarva Papa Kshayakaram Sarva Siddhi Pradayakam || 7 ||

Sarva Rakshakaram Satyam Satyam Sarvagatam Vibhum |
Panchamrita Stavam Pathed Yah Sa Tirtha Phalam Apnuyat || 8 ||

|| Iti Shri Narasimha Panchamrita Stotram Sampurnam ||
hindiMeaning:
अहोबल, गुहाबल, क्रोडाचल, भार्गव, योगानन्द, छत्रवट, पावन, कारञ्ज, कृष्णस्थल — इन नौ स्थानों के नृसिंह भगवान को नमस्कार।

उग्र, वीर, महाविष्णु, सर्वतोमुख ज्वलित, भयंकर किन्तु मंगलकारी, मृत्यु की भी मृत्यु नृसिंह को नमस्कार।

प्रह्लाद को वरदान देने वाले, श्रीश (लक्ष्मीपति), हिरण्यकशिपु के संहारक, विकराल मुख वाले, रौद्र नरसिंह को नमस्कार।

सत्त्व, रजस्, तमस् — ये प्रकृति से उत्पन्न तीन गुण हैं। इन गुणों से अतीत आत्मस्वरूप नृसिंह का मैं नित्य आश्रय लेता हूँ।

वज्र के समान नख, दाँत और देह वाले, महाबली, वज्र से उत्पन्न, कामनापूर्ति करने वाले नृसिंह को भजता हूँ।

योगानन्द नरसिंह का नित्य एकाग्रचित्त से ध्यान करें — वे सर्वपापनाशक और सर्वसिद्धिप्रदायक हैं। जो इस पञ्चामृत स्तव को पढ़ता है, उसे तीर्थफल प्राप्त होता है।
benefits: Sacred five-nectar stotra of Lord Narasimha with nine kshetra vandana
Contains the famous 'Ugram Viram Mahavishnum' protection mantra
Traditionally recited for removal of all sins and grants all siddhis
Equal merit to visiting all holy tirthas
Supreme protection from enemies, fear and death
description: The Narasimha Panchamrita Stotram, known in Sanskrit as नरसिंह पञ्चामृत स्तोत्रम्, is a devotional hymn of praise in praise of Lord Narasimha. This sacred composition is sourced from Narasimha Purana and continues to inspire devotees worldwide. Devotees recite this prayer to supreme protection from enemies, fear and death and contain the famous 'Ugram Viram Mahavishnum' protection mantra. It is also said to equal merit to visiting all holy tirthas. It is especially recommended on Saturday (Shanivar) and during Narasimha Jayanti and Narasimha Chaturdashi for maximum spiritual benefit. At 8 verses long, the recitation takes roughly 4 minutes, making it accessible for regular devotional use. On this page, you can read the complete Narasimha Panchamrita Stotram in Devanagari Sanskrit with English transliteration, Hindi meaning (arth), and free PDF download for offline recitation.
seoDescription: Read Narasimha Panchamrita Stotram in Sanskrit with Hindi meaning. Five-nectar hymn of Lord Narasimha with Ugram Viram verse.

Nine Narasimha Ahobilam shrines list Andhra Pradesh
Ugram Viram Mahavishnum verse meaning Narasimha
Why is it called Panchamrita stotram five nectars
Lord Rama recited Narasimha stotra Ahobilam Ramayana
Tirth phalam merit equal to all pilgrimages benefit

seoFaqs.question: What is the Narasimha Panchamrita Stotram?
seoFaqs.answer: The Narasimha Panchamrita Stotram (नरसिंह पञ्चामृत स्तोत्रम्) is a powerful eight-verse Sanskrit hymn dedicated to Lord Narasimha, the man-lion fourth avatara of Vishnu. The name 'panchamrita' (five-nectar) refers to its character as a five-fold nectar of praise — combining the kshetra-vandana (salutation to the nine sacred Narasimha shrines), the celebrated Ugram Viram Mahavishnum mantra, the Prahlada-varada salutation, the Vajranakham description of Narasimha's diamond-hard nails, fangs and body, and the Yoganandam meditation. According to traditional sources the stotra is associated with the Narasimha Purana and is also said in regional traditions to have been recited by Lord Rama himself during his pilgrimage to the Ahobilam shrines. (Sources: Narasimha Purana; Ahobilam sthala-purana tradition.)

seoFaqs.question: What are the nine Narasimha kshetras (Nava Narasimha) listed in verse 1?
seoFaqs.answer: The opening verse of the Narasimha Panchamrita Stotram salutes nine sacred Narasimha kshetras: (1) Ahobalam — the principal Narasimha kshetra in Kurnool district, Andhra Pradesh, where Narasimha is said to have manifested from the pillar; (2) Guhabalam — the Narasimha in the cave; (3) Krodachalam — the angry-mountain Narasimha; (4) Bhargavam — Narasimha worshipped by Parashurama; (5) Yoganandam — the meditative blissful Narasimha; (6) Chhatravatam — Narasimha under the umbrella-banyan; (7) Pavanam — the purifying Narasimha; (8) Karanjam — Narasimha at the karanja tree; (9) Shri Krishnasthalam — Narasimha at Krishna's place. These nine forms are physically enshrined at Ahobilam itself as the Nava-Narasimha temples, considered the supreme Narasimha pilgrimage in India. (Sources: Ahobilam sthala-purana; Nava-Narasimha tradition.)

seoFaqs.question: Why is this stotra called Panchamrita (five-nectar)?
seoFaqs.answer: The name Panchamrita refers to the stotra's structure as a five-fold nectar of praise. The five amritas (nectars) are: (1) the Ahobilam Nava-Narasimha kshetra-vandana opening verse; (2) the Ugram Viram Mahavishnum protection verse — perhaps the most famous Narasimha mantra in all of Sanskrit literature, declared by tradition to be the supreme bhaya-haran mantra; (3) the Prahlada-varada-Hiranyakashipu-haram verse describing Narasimha's pastime; (4) the Sattva-rajas-tamas verse establishing Narasimha as beyond the three gunas (the gunatita-atman); (5) the Vajranakham diamond-nail verse describing Narasimha's invincible body. Together these five 'nectars' form a complete stotra-amrita that, according to verse 8, grants the merit of bathing in all sacred tirthas. (Sources: classical Vaishnava commentaries; Narasimha Purana.)

seoFaqs.question: What is the meaning of the Ugram Viram Mahavishnum verse?
seoFaqs.answer: Ugram Viram Mahavishnum jvalantam sarvatomukham, Nrisimham bhishanam bhadram mrityu-mrityum namamy-aham is widely considered the supreme Narasimha mantra of the Hindu tradition. It salutes Narasimha as: ugram (fierce), viram (heroic), maha-vishnum (the great Vishnu), jvalantam (blazing), sarvatomukham (with faces in all directions), bhishanam (terrifying), bhadram (auspicious), mrityu-mrityum (the death of Death). The juxtaposition of bhishanam (terrifying) and bhadram (auspicious) captures the essence of Narasimha — terrifying to the asuras and to the devotee's inner kleshas, but supremely auspicious to those who take refuge. The verse is traditionally recited 11, 21 or 108 times for protection from grave dangers, premature death, asuric persecution, and supernatural disturbance. (Sources: Narasimha Purana; classical bhaya-haran mantra tradition.)

seoFaqs.question: When and how should the Narasimha Panchamrita Stotram be recited?
seoFaqs.answer: The Narasimha Panchamrita Stotram is most powerful when recited on Saturdays (Shanivar — when Narasimha is invoked to pacify Saturn's malefic effects), Swati nakshatra (Narasimha's birth nakshatra), every Chaturdashi tithi, and especially on Narasimha Jayanti (Vaishakha Shukla Chaturdashi, with the actual abhishekam at the sandhya twilight when Narasimha appeared) and Narasimha Chaturdashi. The optimal time is dusk, facing east, before a Narasimha murti or yantra, after lighting a ghee lamp. Pilgrims undertaking the Ahobilam Nava-Narasimha yatra traditionally recite this stotra at each of the nine shrines. For daily nityapaath the stotra takes only four minutes, making it an ideal short Narasimha recitation. (Sources: Ahobilam yatra paddhati; Narasimha sadhana tradition.)

seoFaqs.question: What benefits does the Narasimha Panchamrita Stotram bestow according to verse 8?
seoFaqs.answer: The closing eighth verse of the Narasimha Panchamrita Stotram declares: sarva-rakshakaram satyam satyam sarvagatam vibhum, panchamrita-stavam pathed yah sa tirtha-phalam apnuyat — 'Truly, truly, this all-protecting, all-pervading Vibhu is this stotra; whoever recites this Panchamrita stava obtains the merit of all sacred tirthas (pilgrimages)'. Traditional commentaries extend the benefits to: complete sin-removal (sarva-papa-kshaya), bestowal of all siddhis (sarva-siddhi-pradayaka), protection from enemies, fear and death, the merit of pilgrimage to all nine Ahobilam Narasimha shrines without physical travel, healing of grave illnesses, removal of black-magic, and the supreme phala of Narasimha-loka residence. The double 'satyam satyam' emphasizes the absolute certainty of these fruits. (Sources: Narasimha Panchamrita verse 8; Ahobilam sthala-purana.)
